title: asperula cynanchica subvar. montana
intro: Asperula cynanchica subvar. montana, also known as Mountain Woodruff, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, commonly found in alpine meadows of the European Alps. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Mountain Woodruff include relief from digestive issues, reduction of inflammation, and the ability to aid in the healing of wounds. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate specific health concerns.
